title: fagraea peregrina
intro: Fagraea peregrina, also known as the frangipani tree or the frangipani flower tree, is a tropical plant native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its white, waxy flowers and fragrant scent. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Fagraea peregrina include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against infections. Fagraea peregrina can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by combining the plant's flowers, leaves, or bark with other herbs and plant extracts.
